intro: Nutrition and fitness are built on physics (energy balance) and biology. Validated formulas give you science-backed starting points, adjustable to your individual response. Loading + maintenance dosed by weight. All values updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current.

Teaching and learning creatine supplementation concepts
Quick reference for nutrition and supplement planning
explanation:
**Nutrition and fitness** depend on physics (energy balance) and biology (macronutrients, micronutrients). This calculator uses formulas validated by the scientific community (ACSM, ISSN, NIH).

## How it's calculated

Loading phase + maintenance phase dosed by body weight.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you if values fall outside reasonable parameters.

## Standard macronutrient distribution

| Macronutrient | % Caloric | Grams/kcal |
|---|---|---|
| Protein | 15-35% | 4 kcal/g |
| Carbohydrates | 45-65% | 4 kcal/g |
| Fats | 20-35% | 9 kcal/g |
| Alcohol | — | 7 kcal/g |
| Fiber | Minimum 25-38g/day | — |

## When to consult a registered dietitian

- If you have conditions (diabetes, hypertension, kidney disease) requiring personalized nutrition.
- If you want significant muscle gain or plan to compete in sports.
- If you have disordered eating patterns (binge eating, extreme restriction).
- If you haven't progressed toward your goal for more than 3 months.
- To plan complete vegan or vegetarian nutrition.

## Final notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. All values updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What's the scientific basis for this creatine dosing calculator?
faq.a: Uses formulas validated by major sports science organizations (ACSM, ISSN, NIH). Updated through 2026 with current research.

faq.q: Is creatine dosing one-size-fits-all or personalized?
faq.a: These are evidence-based averages. Individual response varies by genetics, diet, training, and hydration. Start here, then adjust based on your results.

faq.q: Should I consult my doctor before taking creatine?
faq.a: Yes, especially if you have kidney disease, diabetes, or take medications. Creatine is safe for healthy people but requires medical clearance if you have health conditions.
All data and recommendations are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly based on latest research.

faq.q: Do I need a loading phase, or can I just take maintenance dose?
faq.a: Both work. Loading (20g/day for 5-7 days) reaches peak faster but is optional. Maintenance-only dosing (3-5g/day for ~30 days) is more convenient and achieves the same results.

faq.q: How much creatine should I take per day during maintenance?
faq.a: Standard maintenance: 3-5g/day (roughly 0.03g per kg body weight). Consistency matters more than exact timing.
Absolutely. Creatine doesn't affect hormones and provides the same strength and performance benefits as for men.

faq.q: Does creatine really work, or are there better supplements?
faq.a: Creatine has solid scientific evidence for muscle gain and strength. Whey protein also has strong evidence. Many other supplements lack proven benefit. Always prioritize diet and training.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
